mapper.insert: insert into batch.job_instance ( tenant_id, job_definition_id, trigger_request_id, job_code, instance_no, biz_date, trigger_type, instance_status, queue_code, worker_group, priority, dedup_key, version, expected_partition_count, success_partition_count, failed_partition_count, trace_id ) values ( #{tenantId}, #{jobDefinitionId}, #{triggerRequestId}, #{jobCode}, #{instanceNo}, #{bizDate}, #{triggerType}, #{instanceStatus}, #{queueCode}, #{workerGroup}, #{priority}, #{dedupKey}, #{version}, #{expectedPartitionCount}, #{successPartitionCount}, #{failedPartitionCount}, #{traceId} )
mapper.update: update batch.job_instance set instance_status = #{instanceStatus}, success_partition_count = #{successPartitionCount}, failed_partition_count = #{failedPartitionCount}, finished_at = #{finishedAt}, updated_at = current_timestamp where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and id = #{id}

update batch.job_instance set instance_status = #{instanceStatus}, expected_partition_count = #{expectedPartitionCount}, started_at = coalesce(started_at, #{startedAt}), updated_at = current_timestamp where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and id = #{id}
mapper.select: select count(1) from batch.job_instance where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and instance_status in ('WAITING', 'READY', 'RUNNING')
